text: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it. These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions. LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance. The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away. This incredible technology lets us hear the universe's violent symphonies. Want to dive deeper into the cosmos? Like and follow for more!
